sourceNotes: Bihar 2001 scenario split news items (2001-01 to 2005-12)

newsItems.title: Government Announces Panchayat Polls After 23-Year Hiatus
newsItems.description: The Bihar state government announces the schedule for Panchayat elections, marking the first local body polls in the state since 1978. Intense political rivalries ignite in rural districts.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state police to fast-track municipal arrangements and secure polling booths.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims bias in block-level seat reservations, creating local disputes.

reactionOptions.text: Demand deployment of central paramilitary forces to prevent booth capturing by dominant groups.

risk.badOutcome: Delays in central force arrival lead to localized clashes in remote panchayats.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming all-party ward committees to monitor electoral peace at polling centers.

risk.badOutcome: Rival party workers refuse to sit together on panels, causing localized stalemates.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Separation of Jharkhand Triggers State Treasury Crisis
newsItems.description: Following the creation of mineral-rich Jharkhand, the Bihar assembly debates the massive loss of mining revenue and the challenge of paying government salaries.

reactionOptions.text: Send an official cabinet team to Delhi to request a special financial recovery package for Bihar.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministries delay approvals, demanding detailed fiscal reform plans first.

reactionOptions.text: Expose previous state mining lease documents, alleging massive corruption under prior regimes.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling coalition labels the charges as politically motivated slander.

reactionOptions.text: Propose reviewing commercial agriculture and dairy duties to boost internal state revenue.

risk.badOutcome: Farmer unions protest the proposed duties, forcing a rollback of the draft.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Clashes Erupt in Rural Districts Over Panchayat Campaigning
newsItems.description: Pre-election clashes are reported in Central Bihar districts. Rival caste factions mobilize supporters, leading to local security concerns.

reactionOptions.text: Impose Section 144 CrPC prohibitions and arrest history-sheeters to enforce electoral peace.

risk.badOutcome: Local leaders allege targetting of their candidates by district police.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a judicial inquiry into police inaction, alleging state favoritism of ruling candidates.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases videos of opposition cadres instigating the violence.

reactionOptions.text: Propose organizing joint cross-party peace marches through sensitive blocks to defuse caste tensions.

risk.badOutcome: Extreme factions boycott the marches, reducing their impact on the ground.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Panchayat Elections See Historic Turnout Among Marginalized
newsItems.description: Panchayat polling concludes with an unprecedented voter turnout. Margnalized communities vote in large numbers, changing rural power dynamics.

reactionOptions.text: Hail the turnout as a victory for social justice and decentralized democracy in Bihar.

risk.badOutcome: Newly elected sarpanchs complain of lack of office spaces and funds.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state immediately conduct administrative training for the newly elected local leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Training schedules face administrative delays, keeping work stalled.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a direct funding model where 40% of state schemes are managed by village sabhas.

risk.badOutcome: Bureaucratic lobbies block direct funding, citing lack of auditing capacity.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Early Monsoon Rains Spark North Bihar Flood Concerns
newsItems.description: Heavy pre-monsoon showers raise water levels in the Gandak and Kosi rivers. North Bihar districts report waterlogging in low-lying crop zones.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate emergency state funds to reinforce river embankments and prepare rescue boats.

risk.badOutcome: Contractors siphon embankment repair funds, drawing critical local press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CAG audit of embankment works, alleging prior funds were spent on paper only.

risk.badOutcome: District administrations refuse to share files, citing emergency protocols.

reactionOptions.text: Propose constructing permanent elevated concrete shelters in high-risk zones using relief labor.

risk.badOutcome: Land acquisition issues in flood plains stall shelter construction projects.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Opposition Launches Rallies Alleging Rising Ransom Gangs
newsItems.description: BJP and JD(U) hold joint protest rallies in Patna, accusing the RJD government of failing to check organized kidnapping-for-ransom networks.

reactionOptions.text: Form a special police anti-kidnapping task force with fast-track trial powers.

risk.badOutcome: Human rights groups allege procedural excesses during suspect raids.

reactionOptions.text: Expose phone logs and photographs alleging ties between local ruling MLAs and gang leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party files defamation suits, leading to prolonged legal battles.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a state-backed witness protection program to encourage testimony against gangs.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of dedicated budget leaves witness houses understaffed and unsafe.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rural Electrification Demands Grow Amid Summer Outages
newsItems.description: North Bihar rural areas report daily power outages of over 20 hours. Farmers demand cheap electricity for diesel pump alternatives.

reactionOptions.text: Procure extra power from the national grid and set up dedicated feeder lines for rural farming zones.

risk.badOutcome: State electricity board reports heavy fiscal deficits, delaying line works.

reactionOptions.text: Lead farmer sit-ins outside electricity board offices, demanding immediate tariff reductions.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes with security staff lead to arrests of block leaders.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing decentralized rice-husk biomass power plants in remote village clusters.

risk.badOutcome: Private microgrid developers pull out, citing low tariff recovery returns.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rabri Devi Defends RJD Social Justice Record Against Centre
newsItems.description: CM Rabri Devi attacks the central government, accusing it of using central investigating agencies to destabilize the social justice government in Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Hold grand conventions across the state to rally backward class and minority voters.

risk.badOutcome: Counter-mobilizations by forward castes polarize voter dynamics.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the dismissal of the state government, submitting a governor memo on lawlessness.

risk.badOutcome: Parliamentary allies decline support, calling the demand politically premature.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a special assembly debate focusing purely on post-separation resource sharing.

risk.badOutcome: Frequent disruptions and sloganeering prevent the assembly from voting on resolutions.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Primary School Teachers Launch Strike Over Unpaid Salaries
newsItems.description: Thousands of primary school teachers protest outside the Patna secretariat, demanding immediate clearance of unpaid DA and salary arrears.

reactionOptions.text: Release 30% of the arrears immediately, offering treasury bonds for the remaining portion.

risk.badOutcome: Unions reject bonds, continuing blockades of district school inspector offices.

reactionOptions.text: Support the teachers, demanding the government cut its advertising budgets to pay staff.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases salary expenditure audits, calling the strike politically timed.

reactionOptions.text: Propose creating a dedicated escrow account for education funds to prevent redirection of salaries.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department raises legal objections to locking state funds in escrow.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Closure of North Bihar Sugar Mills Sparks Farmer Distress
newsItems.description: Three major sugar mills in Champaran and Darbhanga shut down operations, leaving sugarcane farmers without buyers and demanding government intervention.

reactionOptions.text: Announce state loans to revive the mills and clear pending crop payments for farmers.

risk.badOutcome: Revival works drag on, leaving the current season crops to rot in fields.

reactionOptions.text: Lead farmer protests at mill gates, demanding the state nationalize the closed mills.

risk.badOutcome: Mill owners obtain court stays against state intervention, stalling the demand.

reactionOptions.text: Propose cash subsidies for farmers to switch from sugarcane to maize and pulses.

risk.badOutcome: Farmers express reluctance to abandon traditional crops, leaving subsidies unused.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Barauni Refinery Labor Strike Disrupts Local Fuel Supply
newsItems.description: Contract worker unions at the Barauni oil refinery launch an indefinite strike, demanding job regularization and safety benefits.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state police to protect non-striking staff and ensure refinery operations continue.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between striking workers and police trigger local shutdowns in Begusarai.

reactionOptions.text: Support the workers, introducing assembly resolutions demanding central public sector job guarantees.

risk.badOutcome: Refinery management threatens to shift future expansion budgets to other states.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a tripartite arbitration panel of labor commissioners, union heads, and IOCL management.

risk.badOutcome: Union factions reject the arbitration terms, prolonging the deadlock.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: State Accuses Centre of Withholding Flood Calamity Funds
newsItems.description: The Bihar Finance Minister accuses the central government of releasing inadequate calamity funds, citing biased treatment compared to other states.

reactionOptions.text: Publish a detailed report on central fund allocations and hold protest rallies in Delhi.

risk.badOutcome: Central agencies counter that Bihar failed to submit proper utilization certificates.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CAG audit of relief fund allocations, alleging diversion of central aid to party bases.

risk.badOutcome: Rural voters feel the opposition is trying to block calamity aid, leading to local anger.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a coalition of flood-affected states to demand objective, formula-based relief allocation.

risk.badOutcome: Neighboring states decline joint action due to their own political alliances.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Lalu Prasad Reorganizes RJD Committees to Secure Base
newsItems.description: RJD Chief Lalu Prasad Yadav initiates a massive organizational rejig, prioritizing local OBC and minority representatives in district committees.

reactionOptions.text: Launch extensive membership drives, offering cheap party identity cards to rural youth.

risk.badOutcome: Discontented old cadres resign in protest over seat reallocations.

reactionOptions.text: Expose family ties and crime cases of the new committee members, calling it dynasty politics.

risk.badOutcome: OBC voter organizations interpret the charges as anti-backward bias.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a non-partisan Youth Assembly to discuss educational reforms in the state.

risk.badOutcome: Rival youth wings clash at the inaugural venue, forcing a postponement.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Clears Patna Bypass Expressway Plan
newsItems.description: The urban development department clears the layout for a major bypass expressway around Patna to reduce growing traffic congestion in the capital.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate state funds and award contracts to construct the bypass under strict completion timelines.

risk.badOutcome: Local landowners in Patna outskirts file suits challenging the land valuation.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state provide guaranteed housing plots first to all families displaced by construction.

risk.badOutcome: Urban trade chambers accuse the opposition of blocking civic infrastructure.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) model to attract private developers and save public funds.

risk.badOutcome: Private bidders pull out, citing low toll revenue estimates in the state.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Illegal Sand Mining on Son Riverbed Sparks Alarms
newsItems.description: Environmental groups and local farmers raise alarms over illegal sand mining along the Son riverbed, alleging erosion of riverbanks and drop in water tables.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy armed mining guards, impose fines, and seize all unregistered excavators at the sites.

risk.badOutcome: Mining mafia attacks the guard posts, leading to local law and order concerns.

reactionOptions.text: Expose cabinet minister letters recommending mining permit exemptions to specific contractors.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party claims the letters are forged, filing counter police cases.

reactionOptions.text: Propose transferring mining rights to local panchayat-run sand cooperatives to secure local revenue.

risk.badOutcome: Administrative delays in registry leave the cooperatives inactive for months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Merchant Robberies Trigger Trade Strike in Muzaffarpur
newsItems.description: A series of armed robberies targetting cash traders in Muzaffarpur sparks outrage. Local chambers of commerce announce an indefinite market closure.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy extra police platoons, set up check-posts, and hold meetings with trade leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed arrests lead to trade bodies continuing the market closures.

reactionOptions.text: Lead merchant dharnas outside the SSP office, demanding immediate transfer of district police chiefs.

risk.badOutcome: Trade groups distance themselves, saying they want security, not political battles.

reactionOptions.text: Propose subsidizing private security installations and licensed arms for registered merchant groups.

risk.badOutcome: Police department objects, citing risks of arms misuse in public.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Central Railway Minister Nitish Kumar Announces Bihar Projects
newsItems.description: Union Railway Minister Nitish Kumar announces several mega projects for Bihar, including coach factories and new lines, heating up the state development debate.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the Centre transfer development funds to the state for coordinate land acquisitions first.

risk.badOutcome: Delays in state land clearance are highlighted by central ministers as anti-growth.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign on the central railway projects, highlighting the NDA's commitment to industrializing Bihar.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party releases data showing slow progress of previously announced lines.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint board representing state planners and railway officials to synchronize urban bypasses.

risk.badOutcome: Jurisdictional disputes between state and central boards stall the project meetings.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Pre-Monsoon Showers Clog Patna Drainage System
newsItems.description: Sudden heavy showers clog Patna's primary drainage lines. Major residential and commercial areas report waist-deep waterlogging.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy emergency pumping sets, clear municipal clogs, and suspend negligent municipal engineers.

risk.badOutcome: Frequent power cuts during showers stall pumping works, causing public anger.

reactionOptions.text: Demand an independent probe into municipal expenditure, alleging siphoning of drain desilting funds.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party blames the rapid unplanned urban growth under previous central schemes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose hiring international consultants to design a permanent gravity-based drainage master plan.

risk.badOutcome: Master plan designs require years of survey, leaving the current season unresolved.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Embankment Breach in Kosi Region Deluges 100 Villages
newsItems.description: A sudden breach in the Kosi river embankment in Saharsa district submerges over 100 villages, displacing thousands of families overnight.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state rescue boats, distribute dry grain packets, and set up medical camps in elevated spots.

risk.badOutcome: Shortage of country boats delays rescue in interior hamlets, drawing local criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a judicial inquiry into embankment safety audits, alleging negligence in structural maintenance.

risk.badOutcome: Audit processes delay the release of urgent local relief works, angering victims.

reactionOptions.text: Propose constructing raised mud platforms (chauras) to safeguard cattle and families during seasonal breaches.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed execution keeps the raised platforms unfinished before next monsoons.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Alleged Diversion of PDS Wheat Sparks Auditing Row
newsItems.description: Auditing officers report irregularities in state food storage yards, alleging that central PDS wheat allocations were diverted to private mills.

reactionOptions.text: Suspend suspected warehouse managers, order police FIRs, and mandate digitized stock ledgers.

risk.badOutcome: Warehouse unions strike in protest, disrupting current foodgrain supplies.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CBI investigation, alleging that high-level department heads are complicit in the diversion.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party accuses the opposition of trying to create administrative panic.

reactionOptions.text: Propose delegating ration card verification and shop monitoring directly to block panchayat heads.

risk.badOutcome: Local political blockings prevent regular panchayat audit meetings.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bhagalpur Silk Weavers Protest Power and Credit Scarcity
newsItems.description: Traditional silk weavers in Bhagalpur stage protest rallies, demanding dedicated power supply lines and interest waivers on handloom loans.

reactionOptions.text: Announce interest waivers on cooperative loans and subsidize power tariffs for handloom units.

risk.badOutcome: State cooperative banks delay credit rollouts, keeping weavers in distress.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government set up state-run marketing centers to eliminate exploitation by middlemen.

risk.badOutcome: Middlemen cartels threaten to stop buying, stalling local trade transactions.

reactionOptions.text: Propose establishing a modern integrated silk industrial park under a public-private partnership.

risk.badOutcome: High land acquisition costs delay park development planning.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: LJP Holds Massive Rallies Demanding Scheduled Caste Reforms
newsItems.description: The Lok Janshakti Party (LJP) led by Ram Vilas Paswan holds rallies in Patna, demanding special recruitment drives for scheduled castes and police reforms.

reactionOptions.text: Promise to fill all pending SC backlog vacancies in state departments within six months.

risk.badOutcome: General category employee unions protest, threatening strike actions.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the creation of a statutory SC Welfare Board with judicial powers to probe atrocities.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party argues the current commission is sufficient, stalling the bill.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a high court judge-led commission to review reservation compliance across departments first.

risk.badOutcome: Commission hearings drag on, delaying active recruitment drives.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Land Ownership Disputes Trigger Clashes in Jehanabad
newsItems.description: Tensions between agrarian unions and land-owning factions erupt into armed clashes in rural Jehanabad. State police seal district borders.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state special task forces and establish permanent police pickets in sensitive hamlets.

risk.badOutcome: Local farm workers allege police harassment and search excesses.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state fast-track redistribution of surplus ceiling lands to landless laborers.

risk.badOutcome: Land-owning organizations file court challenges, freezing the distributions.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up localized arbitration tribunals with representatives from both sides to resolve titles.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of historical registry documents makes title arbitration impossible, stalling work.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: PHC Audits Expose Shortage of Doctors in Rural Blocks
newsItems.description: A state health department audit reveals that over 60% of rural Primary Health Centers (PHCs) are operating without permanent doctors.

reactionOptions.text: Mandate three years of rural service for all state medical college graduates and fill PHC vacancies.

risk.badOutcome: Medical students strike in Patna, protesting against the compulsory posting rule.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the government double the salaries of rural doctors and provide safe residential quarters.

risk.badOutcome: Finance department rejects the pay hike, citing treasury deficit.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up telemedicine links connecting rural PHCs to district referral hospitals.

risk.badOutcome: Frequent power cuts and poor internet make telemedicine terminals non-functional.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: BJP and JD(U) Hold High-Level Alliance Coordination Meetings
newsItems.description: Seeking to challenge the 13-year RJD regime, leaders of the BJP and JD(U) hold seat-sharing talks to present a single opposition front.

reactionOptions.text: Dismiss the alliance as opportunistic, highlighting the developmental records of the RJD.

risk.badOutcome: Cadres in urban constituencies express concerns over rising opposition coordination.

reactionOptions.text: Jointly announce seat sharing and pledge to end administrative corruption if voted to power.

risk.badOutcome: Factional leaders in three districts rebel, filing independent nominations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a code of campaign ethics to restrict personal attacks and focus rallies on infrastructure.

risk.badOutcome: Rivals ignore the code, escalating personal accusations on stage.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Expands Old Age Pension for Landless Laborers
newsItems.description: CM Rabri Devi announces an expansion of the Old Age Pension scheme, specifically targeting landless agricultural laborers and widows in rural blocks.

reactionOptions.text: Increase the pension budget and set up village panchayat committees to identify eligible families.

risk.badOutcome: Local middlemen demand commissions from beneficiaries, drawing critical press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand biometric database verification to stop cash siphoned by non-existent beneficiaries.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of biometric devices delays pension payouts for months, angering elders.

reactionOptions.text: Propose transferring all pension payouts directly through local post office accounts.

risk.badOutcome: Rural post offices report heavy staff shortages, stalling account openings.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Senate Elections Trigger Patna University Student Clashes
newsItems.description: Tensions during the Patna University senate poll campaign erupt into clashes between rival student groups, forcing police to execute lathi-charges.

reactionOptions.text: Suspend the senate elections, restrict political entry, and deploy police guards at hostel gates.

risk.badOutcome: Student unions unite to launch a strike, alleging suppression of democratic rights.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the police lathi-charge, demanding the immediate release of all arrested student leaders.

risk.badOutcome: Parent associations criticize the party for supporting rowdy campus groups.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming a student-teacher welfare panel to monitor senate campaign funding limits.

risk.badOutcome: Student groups reject funding limits, refusing to cooperate with the panel.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Clears Mahatma Gandhi Setu Pipeline Repairs
newsItems.description: The cabinet clears a special budget to repair the Mahatma Gandhi Setu pipelines, crucial for linking water and transit between North and South Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Award fast-track repair contracts and restrict heavy truck transit to ensure structural safety.

risk.badOutcome: Truck associations strike, halting freight movement across the Ganges.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CBI investigation into previous repair contracts, alleging massive siphoning of state funds.

risk.badOutcome: Commuters criticize the opposition, stating repair works should not be politicized.

reactionOptions.text: Propose launching state-run ferry services for passengers during bridge repair hours.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of river ports near Patna delays ferry launches for months.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: RJD Holds Historic 'Lathi Rally' in Patna Gandhi Maidan
newsItems.description: RJD Chief Lalu Prasad Yadav organizes a massive 'Lathi Rally' in Patna, drawing lakhs of rural supporters carrying lathis to symbolize grassroots empowerment.

reactionOptions.text: Hail the rally as a display of subaltern pride and defiance against elite political conspiracies.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition files petitions in court, alleging misuse of state buses for transport.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the rally, arguing it projects muscle politics and frightens urban citizens.

risk.badOutcome: Rural voter blocks interpret the criticism as an attack on their presence in capital.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that all future rallies be held on development parameters, banning weapons or lathis.

risk.badOutcome: Rivals mock the proposal, continuing traditional rally styles.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Debates Heat Up in Assembly Over Lathi Rally Expenditures
newsItems.description: Following the Lathi Rally, opposition MLAs disrupt assembly proceedings, demanding details of the public funds and transport utilized for the event.

reactionOptions.text: Present audited bills showing the event was fully funded by party donations, defending the turnout.

risk.badOutcome: Discrepancies in bus hiring records draw critical local newspaper coverage.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that RJD refund state transport corporations for the special trains used by rally attendees.

risk.badOutcome: Railway board states special trains are standard paid commercial runs.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party assembly audit panel to clear all doubts regarding public expenditure.

risk.badOutcome: MLAs refuse to sit on the panel, stalling the auditing process.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Farmers Block Rohtas Highways Demanding Paddy Buying Centers
newsItems.description: Paddy farmers in Rohtas district block national highways, protesting the lack of government buying centers and exploitation by local grain agents.

reactionOptions.text: Open emergency state buying centers in Rohtas and guarantee minimum support prices.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed payments from state coop banks trigger fresh sit-ins.

reactionOptions.text: Lead farmer marches to district collectorates, demanding a complete ban on private middleman trading.

risk.badOutcome: Private trading lobbies suspend buying, leaving farmers without immediate cash.

reactionOptions.text: Propose issuing digitized buying slips to prevent private agents from using farmer credentials.

risk.badOutcome: Frequent power failures at rural centers keep system terminals offline.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Sanitation Failures Trigger Major Dengue Outbreak in Gaya
newsItems.description: Over 500 cases of dengue are reported in Gaya. Local hospitals face acute shortage of blood platelets and beds, sparking public panic.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy medical teams from Patna, subsidize platelet kits, and launch district-wide fogging operations.

risk.badOutcome: Substandard fogging chemicals lead to complaints and poor mosquito control.

reactionOptions.text: Expose pile-ups of waste and broken generators in Gaya hospitals, accusing the government of collapse.

risk.badOutcome: Hospital staff unions protest, accusing the opposition of defaming field workers.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a district health board with local doctors to manage emergency logistics.

risk.badOutcome: Disagreements over board leadership delay supply distributions.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Opposition Accuses Government of Bias in Police Postings
newsItems.description: Opposition parties hold a joint press meet, releasing lists of recent police station officer (SO) appointments to allege systematic caste bias.

reactionOptions.text: Assert that the postings reflect state representation and backward class empowerment goals.

risk.badOutcome: Accusations of neglecting merit trigger protests by student bodies in Patna.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a transparent merit-based posting policy monitored by a police selection board.

risk.badOutcome: Backward class voter organizations interpret this as an attempt to restore elite hegemony.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party legislative committee to review posting rules in line with court directives.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed hearings prevent the committee from submitting its report.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cabinet Approves Entertainment Tax Waivers for Regional Films
newsItems.description: To promote Bhojpuri and Maithili languages, the state cabinet approves entertainment tax waivers for films shot locally inside Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Launch a regional film development fund and clear plans to build a film city in Rajgir.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of immediate infrastructure in Rajgir leaves projects on paper only.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that state funds be spent on primary school roofs instead of commercial film subsidies.

risk.badOutcome: Regional artists union criticizes the opposition as anti-cultural.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that tax-exempt films screen educational history chapters for free at rural schools.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of projector facilities in rural schools prevents the screens.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Anti-Bihari Attacks in Assam Trigger Student Protests in Patna
newsItems.description: Reports of attacks on Bihari railway candidates in Assam trigger angry student demonstrations in Patna, causing minor rail blockades.

reactionOptions.text: Contact the Assam government to demand safety measures and deploy police to clear Patna rail tracks.

risk.badOutcome: Rail clearing leads to clashes with student groups near Patna junction.

reactionOptions.text: Lead student dharnas outside central railway offices, demanding reserve quotas for local state candidates.

risk.badOutcome: Railway ministry rejects regional quotas, citing national recruitment rules.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party delegation to travel to Assam to coordinate peace and student dialogues.

risk.badOutcome: Political groups in Assam refuse to host the delegation, stalling peace talks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kidnapping of Patna Doctor Sparks IMA Strike Notice
newsItems.description: A prominent doctor is kidnapped in Patna. The Indian Medical Association (IMA) issues a strike notice, threatening shutdown of all private clinics.

reactionOptions.text: Instruct DGP to coordinate anti-ransom operations, rescue the doctor, and assign guards to clinics.

risk.badOutcome: Extended rescue search leads to IMA executing the private clinic shut-down.

reactionOptions.text: Lead protest rallies with IMA members, demanding immediate rollback of license fees and safer zones.

risk.badOutcome: General public complains about lack of emergency medical access during rallies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a statutory Hospital Security Act to make offenses against doctors non-bailable.

risk.badOutcome: Legal drafting delays keep the bill pending in the assembly secretariat.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: UPA Alliance Talks Begin Ahead of General Elections
newsItems.description: The RJD, Congress, and LJP initiate high-level seat-sharing talks in Patna to present a unified UPA front against the NDA.

reactionOptions.text: Concede five seats to allies to lock down the coalition, announcing joint rallies.

risk.badOutcome: Local party cadres rebel in the ceded seats, filing independent papers.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight contradictions between RJD and LJP on law and order, calling the alliance unstable.

risk.badOutcome: Coalition leaders resolve differences quickly, reducing the impact of charges.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the coalition draft a written manifesto focusing purely on state finance recoveries.

risk.badOutcome: Disagreements over specific tax sharing delay the manifesto launch.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Government Evaluates Charwaha Vidyalaya SHEPHERD Schools
newsItems.description: The education department orders an evaluation of RJD's shepherd school model (Charwaha Vidyalayas), drawing mixed reviews from educationists.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate extra state funds to add vocational computer blocks to successful shepherd schools.

risk.badOutcome: Audit reports expose siphoning of school funds in two districts, drawing bad press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the state convert these schools into regular primary schools with standard infrastructure.

risk.badOutcome: Marginalized sections view this as attempt to close down subaltern spaces.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that local panchayats oversee school attendance and teacher postings directly.

risk.badOutcome: Panchayat heads decline oversight, citing lack of training in school rules.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Lok Sabha Election Campaigns Reach Peak in Bihar
newsItems.description: Campaigning for 40 parliamentary seats heats up. RJD focuses on social justice and secularism, while NDA attacks the state's development record.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign heavily on social empowerment and the representation of backward classes in politics.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition highlights instances of delayed crop payments and rural power cuts.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight 15 years of development deficit, focusing on closed mills and student out-migration.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling candidates win several key seats due to strong regional caste equations.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a series of public debates on attracting private agro-industries to second-tier cities.

risk.badOutcome: Rivals refuse to attend, leading to media campaigns calling them anti-dialogue.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Incidents of Booth Capturing Reported in Saran and Hajipur
newsItems.description: Lok Sabha polling in Saran and Hajipur witness instances of booth capturing. The Election Commission orders re-polling in multiple centers.

reactionOptions.text: Order strict re-polling, deploying extra police forces and installing video recorders.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition accuses the local administration of bias towards the ruling candidates.

reactionOptions.text: Petition the EC to deploy exclusive central paramilitary forces for all re-polling booths.

risk.badOutcome: EC delays approvals, citing logistics of central battalions during multi-phase polls.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that every polling center allow a joint candidate team to inspect EVM seals live.

risk.badOutcome: Cadres clash outside booths despite joint monitoring, causing minor delays.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: UPA Sweeps Bihar Lok Sabha Polls; RJD Wins 22 Seats
newsItems.description: The RJD-Congress-LJP alliance sweeps Bihar, winning 29 out of 40 seats. Lalu Prasad Yadav is sworn in as the Union Railway Minister.

reactionOptions.text: Declare the mandate as a triumph of secular forces and launch state-wide celebration rallies.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition files petitions challenging candidate affidavits in three seats.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight that despite Delhi power, the state administration remains crippled by lack of funds.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party argues the UPA victory will bring massive packages to Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party convention to draft a joint state economic recovery plan to submit to UPA.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce rivalries between state leaders prevent the convention from signing draft.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Barauni Thermal Power Station Shuts Down Operations
newsItems.description: A technical breakdown at the Barauni thermal power station leads to complete power shutdowns, plunging Patna and central Bihar into blackouts.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate emergency funds to replace boiler parts and deploy mobile diesel generators.

risk.badOutcome: Generator fuel bills trigger auditing rows and local press criticisms.

reactionOptions.text: Demand dismantling the corrupt state utility board and importing power from NTPC directly.

risk.badOutcome: Utility board unions strike, threatening to shut down grid lines.

reactionOptions.text: Propose signing a long-term modernization treaty with NTPC to manage state generation plants.

risk.badOutcome: Negotiations stall over employee pension transfer clauses, delaying work.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Kosi and Bagmati Rivers Submerge 20 North Bihar Districts
newsItems.description: Heavy seasonal rain in Nepal triggers massive water releases, causing Kosi and Bagmati rivers to flood 20 districts and displace over 20 lakh people.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy state resources for aerial food drops, and set up medical camps in dry highland areas.

risk.badOutcome: Substandard food packets distributed trigger protest rallies at block offices.

reactionOptions.text: Demand the central government declare it a national disaster and release Rs 1,000 crore relief.

risk.badOutcome: Central delegation delays visits to assess damage, slowing relief flows.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a joint Nepal-Bihar river safety commission to monitor dam releases during monsoon.

risk.badOutcome: Diplomatic protocols delay the commission meetings, keeping monsoon active.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Central UPA Cabinet Announces Rs 500 Crore Bihar Package
newsItems.description: Following visits by UPA ministers, the central government announces a special Rs 500 crore package for Bihar flood relief and infrastructure repairs.

reactionOptions.text: Hail the package as a benefit of UPA coordination and speed up road repair works.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition alleges diversion of central funds to specific political strongholds.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the CAG monitor the package, alleging prior relief funds were siphoned.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party accuses the opposition of trying to block the development funds.

reactionOptions.text: Propose allocating the fund to districts strictly based on population and damage indices.

risk.badOutcome: Delayed damage assessments stall the allocation, leaving funds unspent.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Patna School Student Kislay Kaushal Kidnapped; Public Outrage
newsItems.description: Armed men kidnap school student Kislay Kaushal in Patna. The incident triggers massive public fury and demands for immediate police action.

reactionOptions.text: Order a massive police crackdown on suspected gang hideouts in Patna outskirts.

risk.badOutcome: Extended search fails to rescue the student, drawing critical news channels.

reactionOptions.text: Lead citizen protest rallies in Patna, demanding the CM's resignation over law collapse.

risk.badOutcome: Police resort to tear gas to clear rallies, leading to cadre arrests.

reactionOptions.text: Propose setting up special fast-track courts to try kidnapping suspects within 30 days.

risk.badOutcome: Legal department raises objections to fast-tracking trials, stalling the bill.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Patna Schools and Markets Shut Down in Kidnapping Protests
newsItems.description: Protesting the failure to rescue Kislay Kaushal, trade bodies and school associations call for a complete shutdown of Patna markets and institutions.

reactionOptions.text: Deploy police guards outside all schools and assure trade leaders of speedy investigation.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between student protestors and police trigger minor injuries.

reactionOptions.text: Continue the protest strikes, demanding the home portfolio be transferred to a senior administrator.

risk.badOutcome: General public gets frustrated by school shutdowns, demanding normal classes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an assembly resolution condemning the kidnap and forming a joint peace advisory.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition boycotts the assembly session, calling the resolution a PR stunt.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Opposition Submits Memo to Governor Demanding President's Rule
newsItems.description: NDA leaders meet the Governor of Bihar, submitting a memorandum on the law and order state and demanding the imposition of President's Rule.

reactionOptions.text: Dismiss the demand as undemocratic, asserting that the state government enjoys a clear majority.

risk.badOutcome: Governor sends a report to Centre on law and order, creating administrative tension.

reactionOptions.text: Hold state-wide dharnas, calling the regime 'Jungle Raj' and demanding central intervention.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party cadres disrupt the dharna spots, leading to localized clashes.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a special debate on law and order in the assembly to clear all allegations.

risk.badOutcome: Rival slogans disrupt the assembly debate within minutes, stalling the house.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Police Launch Major Crackdown on Ransom Hideouts
newsItems.description: Under severe public pressure, state police launch coordinate raids in Patna, Nalanda, and Vaishali, arresting several gang members.

reactionOptions.text: Publicize the arrest details on state media, promising safety for all school children.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition claims the main gang leaders were tipped off and escaped.

reactionOptions.text: Label the crackdown as cosmetic, alleging it is timed to mislead voters before assembly polls.

risk.badOutcome: IMA welcomes the police actions, reducing the impact of criticism.

reactionOptions.text: Propose safety committees in every district with schools, parents, and police reps.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of police staff prevents them from attending committee meetings.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Assembly Campaigning Reaches Peak; UPA Alliances Split
newsItems.description: With assembly polls scheduled, RJD and LJP decide to fight independently, turning the election into a three-cornered contest with the NDA.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign heavily on social justice legacy, warning against upper-caste NDA resurgence.

risk.badOutcome: LJP candidate campaigns split the secular vote share in key blocks.

reactionOptions.text: Highlight administrative paralysis and road deficits, offering a development roadmap.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling party rallies counter with social justice appeals, keeping vote tight.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that all parties sign a draft pledging to support infrastructure regardless of mandate.

risk.badOutcome: Rival candidates refuse to sign, calling it pre-election posturing.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Bihar Assembly Elections Result in Hung House
newsItems.description: The election results deliver a hung assembly. NDA wins 92 seats, RJD 75, and LJP 29, leaving no coalition with a clear majority to form government.

reactionOptions.text: Hold talks with independent MLAs to secure support, asserting RJD's right to form cabinet.

risk.badOutcome: LJP refuses to support any RJD-led cabinet, keeping the deadlock active.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the Governor invite the NDA as the single largest pre-poll alliance.

risk.badOutcome: Governor refuses to invite NDA, citing lack of signed letters from allies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose an all-party round table to explore a neutral compromise cabinet for the state.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce leader rivalries prevent the round table from meeting on schedule.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: President's Rule Imposed in Bihar; Rabri Devi Steps Down
newsItems.description: Following the deadlock, Governor Buta Singh recommends President's Rule. The Union Cabinet approves; CM Rabri Devi formally steps down.

reactionOptions.text: Pledge cooperation with Governor's administration while preparing cadres for fresh polls.

risk.badOutcome: Cadres express frustration over loss of administrative access in districts.

reactionOptions.text: Label the President's Rule as a backdoor RJD regime run by UPA allies from Delhi.

risk.badOutcome: Governor issues administrative transfers of corrupt block officers, reducing impact.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming a bipartisan advisory council of ex-MLAs to assist the Governor.

risk.badOutcome: Governor administration declines, citing direct bureaucratic control rules.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Rumours of LJP Defections Spark Horse-Trading Accusations
newsItems.description: Local news channels report that RJD is trying to win over LJP MLAs to form a majority, leading to fierce accusations of horse-trading.

reactionOptions.text: Deny the claims, stating that any political alignment will be transparent and legal.

risk.badOutcome: Audio clips of alleged MLA negotiations leak, drawing critical press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand that the Governor freeze assembly proceedings and disqualify defecting MLAs.

risk.badOutcome: Legal experts state that anti-defection rules only apply inside the house.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that all parties sign a public pledge to not accept defecting MLAs in their ranks.

risk.badOutcome: Rivals dismiss the pledge as posturing, continuing private talks.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Cabinet Dissolves Bihar Assembly; Legal Challenges Filed
newsItems.description: On Governor Buta Singh's recommendation, the Union Cabinet dissolves the Bihar Assembly overnight. NDA files immediate challenges in Supreme Court.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the dissolution as a necessary step to prevent horse-trading and clean the mandate.

risk.badOutcome: Supreme Court issues critical remarks during preliminary hearings.

reactionOptions.text: Condemn the dissolution as murder of democracy, calling for state-wide black day rallies.

risk.badOutcome: Clashes between cadres during rallies draw police security actions.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that all parties pass a resolution demanding fresh elections be held within 90 days.

risk.badOutcome: EC states poll schedules depend on monsoon and security forces availability.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Supreme Court Begins Hearings on Assembly Dissolution Cases
newsItems.description: A constitution bench of the Supreme Court begins hearings on petitions challenging the dissolution of the Bihar assembly, drawing national focus.

reactionOptions.text: Submit detailed Governor security intelligence reports defending the dissolution decision.

risk.badOutcome: SC judges question the validity of intelligence reports, causing bad press.

reactionOptions.text: Demand adherence to SR Bommai rules, asserting that majority must only be tested on floor.

risk.badOutcome: Ruling allies claim the Bommai precedent does not apply to un-constituted assemblies.

reactionOptions.text: Propose a parliamentary bill to explicitly define Governor powers regarding assembly dissolution.

risk.badOutcome: National parties delay debates, leaving the current legal case unresolved.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Governor Orders Direct Audits of School Mid-Day Meals
newsItems.description: The Governor's administration orders district collectorates to audit primary school mid-day meals, exposing leakage of food grain allocations.

reactionOptions.text: Enforce audit recommendations, cancel supplier licenses, and register police cases.

risk.badOutcome: Supplier lobbies strike, disrupting meal distributions in three districts.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a CBI probe into previous meal budgets, alleging RJD siphoned development aid.

risk.badOutcome: RJD claims the audit is a politically motivated hunt by UPA opponents.

reactionOptions.text: Propose delegating meal cooking and monitoring to local school mother committees.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of basic kitchen sheds in rural schools prevents local cooking.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Governor Administration Fast-Tracks Rural Road Projects
newsItems.description: The Governor's administration directs the rural development cell to fast-track pending PMGSY road construction works in Central Bihar.

reactionOptions.text: Allocate matching state funds to clear pending land compensation files for contractors.

risk.badOutcome: Agrarian unions protest, demanding higher land valuation rates.

reactionOptions.text: Demand a probe into prior road delays, alleging contractors paid kickbacks to stay idle.

risk.badOutcome: Contractor associations strike, halting current construction works.

reactionOptions.text: Propose appointing retired engineers to audit road quality before clearing payment files.

risk.badOutcome: Oversight audits delay payments, slowing down overall construction speed.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Fresh Bihar Assembly Elections Announced; NDA Campaigns on Change
newsItems.description: The EC announces fresh assembly elections for October-November. Nitish Kumar projects himself as the chief ministerial face of change and development.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign heavily on social justice defense, warning against upper-caste NDA policies.

risk.badOutcome: Voter fatigue arises over long incumbency, slowing down campaign energy.

reactionOptions.text: Campaign aggressively on development and good governance ('Sushasan'), offering road maps.

risk.badOutcome: RJD candidates counter with local community coordination, keeping seats close.

reactionOptions.text: Propose requesting the EC to install CCTV cameras in all sensitive rural booths.

risk.badOutcome: Technical errors in remote booths leave cameras offline on polling day.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: Supreme Court Declares Assembly Dissolution Unconstitutional
newsItems.description: The Supreme Court declares the assembly dissolution unconstitutional but declines to restore the house, ordering current polls to proceed.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the verdict, stating that the public court will deliver the final political judgment.

risk.badOutcome: Opposition uses the verdict to launch aggressive campaigns against cabinet bias.

reactionOptions.text: Demand resignation of the Union Home Minister and Governor Buta Singh over the SC verdict.

risk.badOutcome: Central ministers refuse to step down, calling the verdict procedural.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the assembly pass a resolution defining clear guidelines for Governor reports.

risk.badOutcome: Electoral campaigns prevent parties from attending joint drafting meetings.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NDA Wins Majority in Bihar; Nitish Kumar Sworn In as CM
newsItems.description: The JD(U)-BJP alliance wins a clean majority of 143 seats, ending 15 years of RJD rule. Nitish Kumar is sworn in as the Chief Minister.

reactionOptions.text: Declare an era of development, and announce immediate plans to clear highway projects.

risk.badOutcome: RJD alleges irregularities in EVM transport in three urban districts.

reactionOptions.text: Accept the mandate, stating that RJD will act as a vigilant opposition to protect social justice.

risk.badOutcome: Local party workers express frustration over losing cabinet influence.

reactionOptions.text: Propose forming a bipartisan state development council to recommend highway priorities.

risk.badOutcome: Fierce leader rivalries prevent the council from holding its first meeting.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.

newsItems.title: NDA Demands Governor Buta Singh Resignation After SC Verdict
newsItems.description: Following the Supreme Court's detailed verdict on dissolution, the state cabinet and NDA leaders demand the immediate resignation of Governor Buta Singh.

reactionOptions.text: Send a formal cabinet memorandum to the President requesting the immediate recall of the Governor.

risk.badOutcome: UPA allies in Delhi delay action, citing administrative procedures.

reactionOptions.text: Defend the Governor's actions, stating he acted to prevent horse-trading and corruption.

risk.badOutcome: SC verdict details draw critical media coverage, weakening the defense.

reactionOptions.text: Propose that the Inter-State Council adopt standard judicial guidelines for governor appointments.

risk.badOutcome: Lack of consensus among states stalls the guidelines draft in Delhi.

reactionOptions.text: No comment.
